intro: St. Brendan's ER, night shift. Maeve Callahan is four years sober and has not told anyone that the number feels less certain than it did this morning.
outro: Two lockers locked. Two things that cannot be unseen by the people who did them. The shift ends. St. Brendan's does not notice.

sections.title: The Long Walk In
sections.what: Maeve arrives for night shift already brittle. The locker holds what she has not touched in four years. She does not open it yet.

sections.title: Triage Arithmetic
sections.what: A held bay forces a divert. Maeve traces Harte's logic in real time and does not like where it leads.

sections.title: Every Small Victory
sections.what: Maeve works cleanly — a difficult airway, a seizing child. Each win feels provisional, credit borrowed against something she already owes.

sections.title: The Death
sections.what: A patient dies on a diverted ward. The triage trail leads directly to Harte's bed-cut. The room absorbs it without a word.

sections.title: Burning
sections.what: Maeve steps outside. The cold air does not help. She stands by her car and then goes back in empty-handed. Not yet.

sections.title: 3 A.M. Draft
sections.what: Theo writes the complaint in the break room. He names Harte, names the death, signs it. Then seals the envelope and locks it away.

sections.title: The Parking Lot
sections.what: Before the shift ends Maeve goes back to her car. Four years end quietly and alone. She walks back in. Nobody saw.
